..summary: ExplorationService interface Javadoc @throws ExplorationException for all four methods, but no interface method signature declares it; SemanticFieldExplorer implementation does declare it — asymmetric contract visibility

evidence: ExplorationService.exploreByRelation (line 36-40): no `throws` clause in signature, but Javadoc says `@throws pl.marcinmilkowski.word_sketch.exploration.ExplorationException if corpus access fails`
Same pattern repeated for exploreMultiSeed (line 47), compareCollocateProfiles (line 58), fetchExamples (line 68) — all document throws in Javadoc but omit from signature
SemanticFieldExplorer.exploreByRelation (line 112): `throws ExplorationException` declared in signature

ExplorationHandlers catches ExplorationException via `catch (ExplorationException e)` on a reference of type ExplorationService — works at runtime since ExplorationException is unchecked, but IDE shows no throws declaration on the interface method, defeating the documented contract
detail.suggestion: Add `throws ExplorationException` to all four ExplorationService interface method signatures. Since ExplorationException extends RuntimeException it is syntactically permitted; adding it to the interface signature makes the documented exception contract visible to callers who hold an ExplorationService reference, consistent with what the implementation already declares.

..summary: MultiSeedExplorer and CollocateProfileComparator declare public constructors/methods on package-private classes, while SingleSeedExplorer correctly uses package-private visibility throughout

evidence: MultiSeedExplorer.java line 31: 'class MultiSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — public constructor

CollocateProfileComparator.java line 27: 'class CollocateProfileComparator' (package-private), line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, GrammarConfig grammarConfig)' — public constructor; line 54: 'public ComparisonResult compareCollocateProfiles(...)' — public method
SingleSeedExplorer.java line 34: 'class SingleSeedExplorer' (package-private), line 41: 'SingleSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)' — package-private constructor (no modifier)
On a package-private class, 'public' on members has no practical effect outside the package — the modifier is misleading about the intended access level
detail.suggestion: Remove the 'public' modifier from MultiSeedExplorer's constructor and CollocateProfileComparator's constructor and methods, making them package-private to match SingleSeedExplorer's convention and accurately signal that these are internal algorithm classes.

..summary: Exploration sub-components depend on the wide QueryExecutor interface instead of narrow SPI ports

evidence: SingleSeedExplorer.java line 37: constructor takes QueryExecutor (the wide interface extending Closeable, CollocateQueryPort, SketchQueryPort, plus executeCqlQuery, executeDependencyPattern, getTotalFrequency)
MultiSeedExplorer.java line 35: 'public MultiSeedExplorer(QueryExecutor executor)' — same wide dependency
CollocateProfileComparator.java line 36: 'public CollocateProfileComparator(QueryExecutor executor, ...)' — same wide dependency
In contrast, api handlers explicitly use narrow ports: SketchHandlers takes SketchQueryPort (line 42), ConcordanceHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort, CorpusQueryHandlers takes CollocateQueryPort
The executeCollocations method used by exploration classes is defined only on QueryExecutor, not on any SPI port — forcing exploration to import the wide interface
detail.suggestion: Extract an ExplorationQueryPort interface (or extend an existing SPI) that exposes only executeCollocations and executeSurfaceCollocations. Declare SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, and CollocateProfileComparator against this narrower type, mirroring the SPI discipline already applied in the api layer.

..summary: SemanticFieldExplorer stores a QueryExecutor field used only in fetchExamples, while exploreByRelation/exploreMultiSeed/compareCollocateProfiles all fully delegate to dedicated sub-components

evidence: `exploreByRelation` delegates entirely to `singleSeedExplorer.explore(...)`
`exploreMultiSeed` delegates entirely to `multiSeedExplorer.findCollocateIntersection(...)`
`compareCollocateProfiles` delegates entirely to `comparator.compareCollocateProfiles(...)`
`fetchExamples` (lines 167-193) is directly implemented on SemanticFieldExplorer itself via `executor.executeBcqlQuery(bcqlQuery, maxExamples)` — breaking the uniform delegation/facade pattern
Private field `private final QueryExecutor executor` (line 54) exists solely to service `fetchExamples`; all other delegation paths never touch it
detail.suggestion: Extract the examples-fetch logic into a dedicated `ExamplesFetcher` package-private class (parallel to SingleSeedExplorer, MultiSeedExplorer, CollocateProfileComparator) and delegate from SemanticFieldExplorer. This makes the facade pattern consistent across all four service methods and removes the dual role of the class.

..summary: executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern silently return empty list for null/empty lemma, while executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException when the lemma is absent from the pattern

evidence: QueryExecutor.java line 36 Javadoc on executeCollocations: 'Returns an empty list silently if null or empty'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line 97-103: 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { logger.debug("..."); return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~153: executeDependencyPattern also guards 'if (lemma == null || lemma.isEmpty()) { return Collections.emptyList(); }'
BlackLabQueryExecutor.java line ~198: executeSurfaceCollocations throws IllegalArgumentException: 'lemma not extractable from pattern — ... (got: ' + bcqlPattern + ')'
All three methods are surface-query variants in the same interface; callers expecting consistent behavior must handle both the silent-empty and the throw cases
detail.suggestion: Unify the missing-lemma contract across all three methods: either (a) make executeSurfaceCollocations return an empty list when no lemma attribute is present (consistent with the null-lemma behavior of siblings), or (b) change executeCollocations and executeDependencyPattern to throw IllegalArgumentException on null/empty lemma (consistent with executeSurfaceCollocations). Document the chosen contract in the QueryExecutor interface Javadoc.

..summary: CLAUDE.md code structure map lists multiple classes at wrong paths or that no longer exist

evidence: CLAUDE.md line 111 lists `api/GrammarConfigSerializer.java` — this class exists only in `src/test/`, not in production sources
CLAUDE.md line 124 lists `exploration/ExplorationService.java` — the actual file is at `exploration/spi/ExplorationService.java`
CLAUDE.md line 133 lists `model/FetchExamplesOptions.java` — the actual file is at `model/exploration/FetchExamplesOptions.java`
CLAUDE.md line 135 lists `model/QueryResults.java` — this file does not exist; result types live under `model/sketch/`
CLAUDE.md line 136 lists `model/RelationType.java` — the actual file is at `config/RelationType.java`
CLAUDE.md lines 157-159 list `viz/Edge.java` and `viz/RelationEdgeType.java` — both actually live under `model/exploration/`
CLAUDE.md line 120 lists `config/RelationPatternBuilder.java` — this file does not exist
CLAUDE.md omits `api/ComparisonResponseAssembler.java`, `api/SketchResponseAssembler.java`, and the entire `query/spi/` package

An older spec section within CLAUDE.md (visible in the embedded concept-sketch-spec content) still references removed packages `grammar/` and `tagging/` and non-existent classes CQLParser, CQLPattern, CQLToLuceneCompiler, WordSketchQueryExecutor, SimpleTagger, LongIntHashMap, CQLVerifier — none of which exist in the current codebase
CLAUDE.md performance-tuning section references `CollocationsBuilderV2` and `HybridLuceneIndexer` — neither exists
detail.suggestion: Regenerate the `## Code Structure` section of CLAUDE.md by walking `src/main/java` and updating all class paths, removing non-existent entries, and adding the missing classes. Remove or clearly archive the stale spec content that references the old `grammar/` and `tagging/` packages.

..summary: SemanticFieldExplorer package-private test constructor accepts executor/nounCqlPattern without null validation, unlike the production injection constructor

evidence: Test constructor (line 70): `SemanticFieldExplorer(QueryExecutor executor, String nounCqlPattern)` has no Objects.requireNonNull calls and no @NonNull annotations on parameters

Production injection constructor (lines 90-100): `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(comparator, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(multiSeedExplorer, ...)`, `Objects.requireNonNull(grammarConfig, ...)` are all present

A null executor passed to the test constructor is silently stored and propagated into CollocateProfileComparator, MultiSeedExplorer, and SingleSeedExplorer, where it will cause an NPE at query time with no descriptive message
detail.suggestion: Add explicit null guards in the test constructor: `Objects.requireNonNull(executor, "executor must not be null")` and `Objects.requireNonNull(nounCqlPattern, "nounCqlPattern must not be null")`. Add @NonNull annotations to both parameters to make the contract visible.

..summary: CqlUtils.findLabelTokenIndex makes two separate passes through the pattern string: one indexOf call to find the label, then a full character scan from position 0 to count bracket tokens

evidence: int labelIndex = pattern.indexOf(labelStr) — first pass to locate label
for (int i = 0; i < pattern.length(); i++) — second pass from position 0 counting '[' characters
The token counter restarts from 0 rather than walking forward from labelIndex
detail.suggestion: Combine into a single forward pass: count '[' characters incrementally while scanning toward labelIndex; stop as soon as the matching bracket is found at or after labelIndex. This eliminates the redundant full-string re-scan.

..summary: executeSurfaceCollocations is declared in both SketchQueryPort and QueryExecutor (which extends it), resulting in duplicate signatures with divergent Javadoc

evidence: SketchQueryPort.executeSurfaceCollocations — concise declaration at line 30
QueryExecutor.executeSurfaceCollocations — re-declared at line 87 with a longer @implNote comment
QueryExecutor already extends SketchQueryPort, so the re-declaration is redundant
detail.suggestion: Remove the re-declaration from QueryExecutor; move any additional Javadoc into the SketchQueryPort definition. If the extended documentation is important, annotate with @see or link to SketchQueryPort from the QueryExecutor interface-level Javadoc.

..summary: ExplorationHandlers error cases — ExplorationException → 503, unknown relation → 400 — are not tested through the HTTP stack

evidence: ExplorationHandlers.sendExplorationError maps ExplorationException → 503 but no test injects a failing service
resolveRelationConfig throws IAE for unknown relation IDs but no test exercises this path via handleSemanticFieldExplore
handleSemanticFieldExploreMulti: no test for seeds.size() < 2 case through the HTTP handler (only service-level validation is tested)
detail.suggestion: Add tests that inject a stub ExplorationService that throws ExplorationException and verify the response is 503. Add tests for unknown relation IDs and single-seed calls to the multi endpoint and verify 400 responses.
